title: rootless: cherry-pick runtime from the system configuration
body:
when creating the default libpod.conf file, be sure the default OCI
runtime is cherry picked from the system configuration.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3781

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

> I think you might have duped some noPivotRoot code... Otherwise looks good

thanks for the quick review.  I've fixed it now

Doesn't work for me.

I removed `~/.config/containers/libpod.con`, `/usr/share/...` has the runtime set to crun but the rootless one still only include the runc array and points the runtime to runc.

@vrothberg how does the `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` file look like?

If that exists, then it overrides the configuration from `/usr/`

> @vrothberg how does the `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` file look like?
> 
> If that exists, then it overrides the configuration from `/usr/`

Doesn't exist. Only the one in /usr/share/...

by default when you build locally, it will use `/usr/local` as prefix.

Are you building like `make podman PREFIX=/usr`?

> by default when you build locally, it will use `/usr/local` as prefix.
> 
> Are you building like `make podman PREFIX=/usr`?

Thanks for clarifying!

LGTM :+1:
LGTM and happy green test buttons.
